text: Everyone believes in the American dream
until it comes true. I was struggling
with this difficulty of my reality which
was that you know when I started out in
the gym which was my first business uh I
didn't have enough money for two rents
and so I had saved up all my money for
two years to start the gym and I didn't
have enough money for rent so I just
slept at the gym which I was fine with
and you know customers basically could
figure out because they could see my
pillow in the corner like they kind of
got what was going on and they were like
good for you for you know pursuing your
dream like that's awesome like keep at
it. And then like the gym started to
grow, right? It started, you know,
started to fill up. We started I started
to be able to hire employees. And you
know, probably 9 months later or a year
later, you know, I'd come in and at this
point I have a manager for location and
it kind of runs not without me, but like
I owned it more than me running, you
know, working inside of it. And I would
show up and then like, you know, the
clients would be like, "Ah, boss man's
here." you know, and I remember being
like, "Wait, I like you guys said that
you were rooting for me to pursue my
dream, and now that I've accomplished my
dream, you guys make snide remarks at
me." And it was, you know, I've said
this before, but it was the first time
that I realized that people want you to
do well, just not better than them. As
you continue to achieve, you will see
that in the vast majority of people.
Now, I believe that special friends
exist who truly want you to win. And if
you have those people, like hold them
close because most people aren't like
that. Uh most people will be happy
either overtly or more likely
subconsciously or you know under the
under the you know under the surface
when you lose they're happy because it
makes them feel better about the things
they didn't take on the risk they didn't
take. And so it's tough. Business isn't
for the faint of heart but at the same
time neither is life. And so I think
like everything everything is hard. And
I think that the only real flaw in the
system is that we have a belief that it
shouldn't be and that if we somehow
change our conditions, it will get
easier. And it basically just never
does. And I think that's been something
that's like helped me kind of get
through my like woe is me wallowing
moments that I have, which is like if I
had a job that I wasn't satisfied with,
I would be unhappy. if I have if I have,
you know, stressors of day-to-day
business that occur like that also can
make me unhappy. Now, of course, we make
the argument nothing makes you happy or
unhappy. Um, but I am human probably
like you and I have things that
definitely have I have better days and
worse days. And I think that more or
less across all of these situations, I
have rem more or less stayed the same,
right? Like I think there are things
that uh money buys you that make life
more convenient. And it's definitely one
of those things that uh you never
believe people until you have it. So I'm
not going to try and dissuade you from
doing it. My whole channel is about
helping you grow your business. Um but I
think that it helped me get over a lot
of stress in thinking that this should
not be this way. Like just like what
what demand of the universe am I making
that isn't true? Business should be
easy. It should feel better. Uh it
shouldn't be this stressful. like it
shouldn't have this many people problems
and also comparing it to the alternative
which also has problems. And so I think
a lot of times we get in this really
negative spiral as business owners,
which is we compare ourselves. We look
at the we look at the grass on the other
side of the field, but we don't know
that it's lined with manure, right?
That's what makes it grow. And I think I
I try to remind myself of that. Like
whenever there's a green pasture, it's
like it's built on.
And so that helps me just say like,
okay, I just don't know what the is on
the other side, but it's there. And that
kind of gets me, you know, kind of back
in the saddle and focused.
